title: MORIE: Methods for Observational Inference and Robust Analysis of Interventions in Scientific Experimentation
description: MORIE is an open-source Python and R package for the analysis of Canadian carceral, police, and oversight data. It implements the DLRM (Doob-Levinsky-Ruhela-Medina) framework as a set of RF modules, spanning provincial OTIS restrictive-confinement microdata, federal Structured Intervention Unit (SIU) reports, the Ontario Special Investigations Unit (Ontario SIU) automated scraper, the Toronto Police Service (TPS) open-data crime categories, the Statistics Canada Crime Severity Index (CSI), and the federal Corrections and Conditional Release Statistical Overview (CCRSO). The framework includes a 10-estimator per-individual causal ensemble (IPW, AIPW, IRM-DML, PSM, PSM-subclassification, AIPW-SuperLearner, PLR), an aggregate IRR family with a GEE clustering grid for high-cardinality groupings, a Doob chi-square family for aggregate contingency tables, a Mandela Rules classifier that operates at both federal (full operationalization) and provincial (duration-only proxy) levels, a Goffmanian institutional-churn analysis suite, a stochastic-physics-of-crime stack (Hawkes self-exciting process with exponential / gamma / Weibull / Lomax kernels, reaction-diffusion, Levy flight, urban scaling, Lotka-Volterra), and an automated scraper for the Ontario SIU director's-report corpus.
references: Sprott, J. B., & Doob, A. N. (2021, February). Solitary Confinement, Torture, and Canada's Structured Intervention Units. Centre for Criminology & Sociolegal Studies, University of Toronto.

Sprott, J. B., Doob, A. N., & Iftene, A. (2021, May). Do Independent External Decision Makers Ensure that 'An Inmate's Confinement in a Structured Intervention Unit Is to End as Soon as Possible'? Schulich School of Law, Dalhousie University.
Doob, A. N. (2020). Affidavit of Anthony N. Doob (Federal Court of Canada, File T-539-20).
United Nations General Assembly (2015). Standard Minimum Rules for the Treatment of Prisoners (Mandela Rules), A/Res/70/175.
Wallace, M., Turner, J., Matarazzo, A., & Babyak, C. (2009). Measuring crime in Canada: Introducing the Crime Severity Index. Statistics Canada Catalogue no. 85-004-X.
Chernozhukov, V., et al. (2018). Double/debiased machine learning for treatment and structural parameters. Econometrics Journal, 21(1), C1-C68.
VanderWeele, T. J., & Ding, P. (2017). Sensitivity analysis in observational research: Introducing the E-value. Annals of Internal Medicine, 167(4), 268-274.
Goffman, E. (1961). Asylums: Essays on the Social Situation of Mental Patients and Other Inmates. Anchor Books.
notes: MORIE is composed of distinct license tiers per memory: analytical modules MIT/Apache (clew MIT/Apache for adoption); MORIE itself follows GPL-2.0-only (Linus copyleft; see LICENSE); the Ontario SIU scraper is additionally subject to the Ontario open-government licence on the source corpus.
